key_differentials: Rogers (AVL, 6.7% own) — AVL host Man City in Guardiola's farewell, open high-event game, sub-8% EO so any return is net rank gain
Mateta (CRY, 7.0% own) — nailed Palace #9 with pride to play for, high-ceiling forward differential
Bruno G. (NEW, 6.7% own) — reliable minutes and attacking returns at home vs struggling WHU, safe-floor differential
Munoz (CRY, sub-8% own) — attacking full-back differential with set-piece and bombing-forward upside
summary: AGGRESSIVE DIFFERENTIAL build for the GW38 finale. The template is hollow this week — Haaland (62.5%) and Gabriel (38%) are both CONFIRMED OUT and blank, erasing the must-own shield, so I lean hard into sub-8% EO punts to climb. Captain is Bowen (WHU) at EV 12.41, 53.6% haul probability — a nailed talisman in a must-win relegation decider with max motivation, a contrarian armband (only ~8% captaincy) versus the field pivoting toward B.Fernandes. Vice is Mbeumo (BRE, full-strength chasing Europe). Differential core: Rogers + Watkins (AVL double-up on the open Man City fixture), Mateta, Bruno G. and Munoz, all under 8% owned. Salah anchors the floor at home vs nothing-to-play-for Palace. Avoided Haaland and Gabriel entirely. Projected ~78.6 xPts with a high ceiling if the AVL/WHU punts land.
